question_text: A wooden block slides across a wooden table. Which direction does the friction force act?

options.label: A
options.text: In the same direction as the block is moving

options.label: B
options.text: Opposite to the direction of the block's motion

explanation: Friction always opposes motion, so if the block is sliding to the right, friction acts to the left. This is why friction slows things down—it acts against the direction they are moving.
